title: Lesson Quiz: Responsible Credit Use and Debt Management
description: Assessment of the concept that Avoiding debt traps and managing repayment strategies.

choices.text: Borrowing in a way that can be repaid on time and managed safely
choices.feedback: Correct. Responsible credit use means borrowing with a realistic repayment plan.
Responsible borrowing should include checking repayment ability.

choices.text: Using new debt to ignore older debt
choices.feedback: That is a warning sign of weak debt management.

choices.text: Treating borrowed money as if it never needs to be repaid
choices.feedback: Borrowed money still creates repayment responsibility.

questions.prompt: Borrowing again to cover older debt can be a warning sign of weak debt management.

choices.feedback: Using new debt to cover old debt can be a sign that debt is no longer being managed safely.
Correct. That pattern can create deeper debt problems over time.

questions.prompt: Which repayment pattern is stronger for long-term financial stability?

choices.text: Skipping payments whenever money feels tight and adding new borrowing later
choices.feedback: That pattern can increase long-term financial risk.
Strong repayment decisions compare consequences before acting.

choices.text: Ignoring due dates as long as some borrowing remains available
choices.feedback: Ignoring due dates weakens debt management.

choices.text: Following a repayment plan and paying on time
choices.feedback: Correct. A clear repayment plan is the stronger strategy for debt management.

questions.prompt: Why is repayment planning important before using credit?

choices.text: Because repayment planning should only happen after the credit is already used
choices.feedback: Repayment planning is strongest before borrowing, not only after the credit is already used.

choices.text: Because repayment ability does not matter if the credit is approved
choices.feedback: Approval alone does not mean the debt will be manageable.

choices.text: Because repayment planning helps compare current borrowing with future ability to manage the debt
choices.feedback: Correct. A strong credit decision checks future repayment ability before borrowing.

choices.text: Because long-term consequences should be ignored in favor of short-term access
choices.feedback: Strong borrowing decisions should consider long-term consequences.

questions.prompt: A debt decision can seem helpful in the short term but still create long-term problems.

choices.feedback: Short-term help can still lead to long-term repayment problems.
Correct. Borrowing decisions should be evaluated for both immediate benefit and long-term consequences.
